meta.source: Greek Web Development Market: Service Tiers, Pricing & Quality Benchmarks for Static Promotional Websites (2024–2026)
meta.scope: Multi-page static/promotional marketing websites (5–10 pages, no e-commerce)
meta.targetClient: Small boutique business (travel agency, legal office, local services SME) seeking first-time online presence

tiers.name: WordPress Quick Job (Template Flip)
tiers.description: Functional website delivered by swapping content into a purchased premium WordPress theme. No custom code, no QA process, no design mockups.
technologyStack: Contact Form 7 or built-in theme form
tiers.clientEditability: WordPress admin (fragile — Elementor/Divi shortcodes trap content)
tiers.idealClient: First-time SME with budget-only mindset; needs a 'business card' online; no SEO ambitions; expects minimal traffic.
tiers.typicalProvider: IT generalists, marketing agencies upselling websites, students, Fiverr/Freelancer.gr low-bid responders
pros: Lowest upfront cost
Fastest delivery (1–3 days)
Client gets a website immediately
Familiar backend for basic edits
cons: Security liability from day one
Performance penalty baked into the platform
High probability of rebuild within 2–3 years
No meaningful SEO foundation
Vendor lock-in to page builder shortcodes
No documentation or handoff

tiers.name: WordPress Semi-Custom (Professional Freelancer / Small Agency)
tiers.description: Professional WordPress build with child theme, page builder, SEO plugin, caching, and a structured client feedback loop. Delivers visible professionalism over Tier 1.
technologyStack: Child theme on lightweight starter (GeneratePress, Kadence, Blocksy)
Managed WP hosting (SiteGround, Kinsta) or quality shared hosting
tiers.clientEditability: WordPress + Elementor (semi-usable; clients frequently break layouts editing)
tiers.idealClient: Small business wanting basic professionalism and basic SEO; values a structured process with mockup sign-off.
pros: Structured process with mockup sign-off
SEO plugin configuration (Yoast green lights, XML sitemap, Search Console)
Faster load times via caching and CDN
Child theme protects customisations from updates
Client can edit content independently
cons: Core Web Vitals still compromised by PHP rendering and JS bloat
15–25 plugin attack surface
No CI/CD, version control, or staging
Content locked in page builder ecosystem
Server-dependent architecture

tiers.name: Next.js Quick Delivery (No CMS)
tiers.description: High-performance static Next.js site with hardcoded content. Blazing speed and zero attack surface, but the client cannot edit anything without the developer.
technologyStack: Static export or Vercel Hobby deployment
Content hardcoded in components or local JSON/MDX
Resend or Formspree for contact form
tiers.clientEditability: none — all content is hardcoded; every change requires a developer
tiers.idealClient: Tech-comfortable client with static, rarely-changing content; performance-focused; willing to depend on developer for any changes.
tiers.typicalProvider: Full-stack JavaScript developers (rare at this spec in the Greek domestic market)
pros: Lighthouse 90–100 mobile performance
Zero plugin attack surface
€0 hosting on free CDN tiers
No monthly licensing fees
CDN edge delivery — 99.9%+ uptime
Future-proof: any React developer can maintain
cons: No CMS dashboard — client cannot edit anything independently
Every content change is a billable developer event
No mockup sign-off or QA checklist in typical delivery
Client dependency creates operational friction for frequently-changing content

tiers.name: Next.js Professional with Sanity CMS
tiers.description: Professional-grade Next.js build with headless CMS, full SEO setup, accessibility baseline, reusable component library, and a clean content editing experience for the client.
technologyStack: Next.js Metadata API + Open Graph + XML sitemap + robots.txt + JSON-LD structured data
Core Web Vitals optimisation (next/image, next/font, lazy loading)
Vercel Pro or Cloudflare Pages hosting
tiers.clientEditability: Sanity Studio — clean, browser-based visual editor; client edits text, images, and metadata independently
tiers.idealClient: SME with growth plans; needs content independence from developer; 3+ year horizon; values performance and SEO.
tiers.typicalProvider: Senior Next.js developers, rare specialist agencies
pros: Client edits content independently via Sanity Studio
Content schema separate from presentation — no technical debt from hardcoded content
Organised codebase any developer can pick up
Complete SEO: JSON-LD, Open Graph, XML sitemap, Core Web Vitals
WCAG AA accessibility baseline
Near-zero ongoing overhead — most cost-efficient for 3+ year ownership
cons: Higher upfront cost than Tier 1–3
Requires a senior developer with Next.js + CMS integration experience
Client may not fully understand what they bought (mitigated by training session)

tiers.name: Next.js Enterprise-Grade with CI/CD, Testing & Security Hardening
tiers.description: Full professional project: everything in Tier 4 plus automated test suites, CI/CD pipeline, security hardening, staging environment, and comprehensive documentation. CMS dual-path: start with Sanity, migrate to self-hosted Payload CMS if needed.
technologyStack: GitHub Actions CI/CD (lint, typecheck, tests, Lighthouse CI, npm audit, deploy)
HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
Snyk or npm audit for CVE detection
Full documentation (README, component docs, deployment runbook, client handoff guide)
CMS dual-path: Sanity.io (start) → Payload CMS self-hosted on Hetzner (future migration)
tiers.idealClient: Regulated or high-trust industry (legal, financial, medical); SEO-critical launch; long-term product expansion plans; multiple content stakeholders; handoff to internal dev team.
tiers.typicalProvider: Extremely rare in Greece; primarily international-facing practitioners who work via Upwork
pros: CI/CD prevents regressions — performance budget enforced automatically
Automated test coverage (60–80% for critical paths)
Security-hardened: CSP, HSTS, dependency scanning, zero secrets in codebase
Full documentation enables team handoff
CMS dual-path: start free (Sanity), self-host later (Payload) for data sovereignty
Staging environment protects production from unreviewed changes
cons: Highest upfront investment
Longer delivery timeline than simpler tiers
Over-engineered for a simple brochure site with no growth plans
Genuinely scarce provider pool in the Greek domestic market
justificationSignals: High-stakes launch where SEO regression has measurable commercial impact
Regulated industry where security incidents have legal/reputational consequences
Long-term product expansion (booking system, client portal, authenticated sections)
Multiple stakeholders needing content approval workflows
Planned handoff to an in-house development team
